The AntMiner T9 is Bitmain’s newest version in the AntMiner T9 series. It boasts a state-­‐of-­‐the-­‐art BM1387
custom-­‐made chip using 16nm technology. All AntMiner T9s are tested and configured prior to shipping to
You must provide your own ATX power supply.
and controller front panel are shown in the following figure:
350mm x 135mm x 158mm Dimensions (L x W x H)
The miner does not contain a DC/DC converter; therefore, higher input voltage will cause higher
Ten PCI-­‐e connectors are located at the top of the AntMiner T9 for connecting the PSU as follows:
Nine PCI-­‐e connectors for the hash boards. Each hash board has a set of three PCI-­‐e connectors.
One PCI-­‐e connector located on the controller. ●
Each hashboard must be powered by the same PSU to prevent possible damage and instability.
1. Connect PSU power cable connectors to each of the nine PCI-­‐e connectors on the top of the AntMiner T9,
ensuring that each hash board is powered by the same PSU.
2. Connect a PSU power cable connector to the AntMiner T9 PCI-­‐e connector on the controller.
3. Connect the network cable to the ETH port.
4. To power up your AntMiner T9, connect the PSUs to the power wall outlet.
If you are using more than one PSU,power up the PSU connected to the controller AFTER you have
The file IPReporter.zip is supported by Microsoft Windows only.
The default DHCP network protocol distributes IP addresses automatically.
4. Right-­‐click IPReporter.exe and run it as Administrator.
5. Select one of the following options:
Shelf, Step, Position – suitable for farm miners to mark the location of the miners.
7. On the controller board, click the IP Report button. Hold it down until it beeps (about 5 seconds).
The IP address will be displayed in a window on your computer screen.
8. In your web browser, enter the IP address provided.
9. Proceed to login using for both the username and password. root
10. In the Network section, you can assign a Static IP address (optional).
1. In Miner Configuration, click General Settings.
2. Set the options according to the following table:
Pool URL Enter the URL of your desired pool.
The AntMiner T9 can be set up with three mining pools, with decreasing
priority from the first pool (pool 1) to the third pool (pool 3).
The pools with low priority will only be used if all higher priority pools are
Worker Your worker ID on the selected pool.
Password The password for your selected worker.
3. Click Save & Apply to save and restart the miner.
To check the operating status of your miner:
2. Under AntMiner, monitor your miner according to the descriptions in the following table:
ASIC# Number of chips detected in the chain.
GH/S(RT) Hash rate of each hash board ( GH/s)
Temp(PCB) Temperature of each hash board (°C).(Applied only to miner with fixed frequency)
Temp(Chip) Temperature of the chips on each hash board (°C).
ASIC status One of the following statuses will appear:
Note:The T9 miner with automatic frequency adjustment firmware will stop running when the
Temp(Chips) reach to 125-­‐135°C,there will be a error message “Fatal Error: Temperature is too
high!” shown in the bottom of kernel log page.

Make sure that the AntMiner T9 remains powered during the upgrade process. If power fails
before the upgrade is completed, you will need to return it to Bitmain for repair.
Select the check box to keep your current settings (default).
Clear the check box to reset the miner to default settings.

5. Click one of the following options:
Reboot -­‐ to restart the miner with the new firmware.
Go Back -­‐ to continue mining with the current firmware. The miner will load the new firmware next
1. In System, click the Administration tab.
2. Set your new password, then click Save & Apply.
1. Turn on the miner and let it run for 5 minutes.
2. On the controller front panel, press and hold the Reset button for 10 seconds.
Resetting your miner will reboot it and restore its default settings. The red LED will automatically
flash once every 15 seconds if the reset is operated successfully.
